etymology.description: Ancient variant of 冂(冂) (jiōng, "borders"), depicts enclosure with 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth") inside -> closed
etymology.interpretation: 冋(冋) (jiōng, "closed") is an ancient variant of 冂(冂), traditionally depicting an enclosed space or area with 口(口) representing an enclosed mouth, symbolizing something that is closed or blocked off.
